‘I feel flat. We have to wake up because if we carry on like this, we should be happy with the Conference League. I don't know why this is happening, I honestly don't know.’

Arne Slot's Liverpool conceded late again as out-of-form Spurs rescued a 1-1 draw at Anfield

Deflated Dominik Szoboszlai refused to hide his frustration and called for his team to 'wake up'

That is the problem. Nobody seems to be able to arrest the slump.

Not Slot, not reigning player of the season Mohamed Salah (a substitute for the first hour), not the best defender in Premier League history Virgil van Dijk. Not any of these big-money players.

It is rinse and repeat: get into a commanding position, fluff several big chances and then capitulate late on. More than 20 times this season, Liverpool have conceded after the 75th minute.

When the clock ticks past that point, the anxiety inside Anfield rises several notches almost as if to say everyone knows what is going to happen, just cut to the chase and get on with it. This stadium has not been a fortress at all this season, more a melting pot of nerves.

How much of the blame should lie at Slot’s feet?

Certainly a high proportion, as is the world of football management. Tudor, for example, was not solely responsible for Antonin Kinsky’s mess-ups in Madrid or his team-mates’ slips midweek but he takes the brunt.

The boos at full-time were louder than they have been at any point of this season as fans have grown sick and tired of the wastefulness at one end and vulnerability at the other. Who can blame them?

They work hard in the week to follow their football team on the weekend. Of course no fan is entitled to seeing their team get results but Liverpool are boring to watch and have been all season. Even when they win, they often do not play as well as we all know they can.

There are a couple of minor positives we must note: the point here actually saw them leapfrog Chelsea, who lost to Newcastle on Saturday, into the top five which will almost guarantee Champions League football.

But do they deserve that? You could say the same about Liam Rosenior’s Chelsea, to be honest, but neither could grumble if they were left to Thursday nights in one of UEFA’s B-grade competitions next year.

Teenager Rio Ngumoha's impressive display was a minor positive for Liverpool on an otherwise disappointing Sunday afternoon, with Slot's men losing vital ground in the top four race

It is also worth saying that Rio Ngumoha, the 17-year-old winger who made his first start in the Premier League here, did more than enough to show he deserves more minutes.

Liverpool are going careful with his body to not ask too much, too soon physically – but he is a supreme talent.

The rest of the attackers were poor, though. Cody Gakpo was unlucky not to make it 2-0 when Guglielmo Vicario tipped a shot on to the post but other than that, the Italian goalkeeper had a relatively quiet second half. Substitutes Salah and Hugo Ekitike were wasteful.

Florian Wirtz, likewise, was largely anonymous and Jeremie Frimpong, starting on Salah’s right wing, was ineffective. Are the above sub-par performances Slot’s fault? Maybe not entirely, but it feels that whoever he plays cannot win games for Liverpool right now.

This is not a new issue either, the Reds have been poor all season. You could say it has been six months but the problem, in truth, goes back further.

Every week, Slot bemoans how his team underperforms against their expected goals (xG) tally but after a certain amount of time, there must be a deeper-lying trouble that is bugging Slot.

The biggest one is the vulnerability his team shows every week. From half-time onwards, the ending was easy to envisage. How many times have a team equalised or scored a winner against them that prompted the reaction ‘that has been coming’?

The champions retreated and Spurs, a team devoid of confidence and staring down the barrel of a shocking relegation, started to fancy their chances.



They are not the first team to do that here this season and judging on the evidence of Slot’s inability to fix the issues, they will not be the last.

Sports.title: Ronnie O'Sullivan wardrobe malfunction sees frustrated snooker star walk out mid-match
Sports.content:
Ronnie O'Sullivan wardrobe malfunction sees frustrated snooker star walk out mid-match

Ronnie O'Sullivan was forced to walk off just minutes into his World Open clash with Ross Muir before returning to secure a 5-1 victory

View 2 Images Ronnie O'Sullivan speaking to the referee before leaving the table. (Image: TNT Sports)

Ronnie O'Sullivan abruptly exited his World Open match against Ross Muir after experiencing a wardrobe malfunction just minutes into the first frame. The Rocket had racked up 15 points but seemed uneasy as he prepared to take a relatively straightforward shot on the black, indicating his issue to the referee.

‌



He attempted to adjust the sponsor patch on his waistcoat and repeatedly tried to proceed with the shot, clearly irritated by the stitching on his attire. Eventually, he'd had enough and, following a brief exchange with the official, stormed out of the hall, causing the first-round match to be suspended.

‌



O'Sullivan shook his head as he departed from the table, leaving Muir bewildered as the Scot was yet to be informed about the problem.

‌



Welsh Open champ who shocked Ronnie O'Sullivan almost quit snooker after breakdown READ MORE :

The seven-time world champion left the venue and returned a few minutes later, after Muir and the audience in Yushan, China, were made aware of the reason for the interruption, and resumed his shot.

A break of 50 aided him in securing the first frame, but there was another pause in play when a fly landed on the cue ball just as Muir was about to strike it.

It was shooed away and subsequently removed from the table during O'Sullivan's shot, who put distractions aside to secure a commanding 5-1 victory.

‌



View 2 Images Ronnie O'Sullivan beat Ross Muir in the first round of the World Open (Image: VCG via Getty Images)

He claimed the first frame and then quickly established a 3-0 lead with breaks of 83 and 69, before Muir executed a strong clearance to win the fourth frame. It proved the last significant contribution from the world No. 109, however, as the Rocket raced to victory in ruthless style.

He appeared destined for a record-breaking 18th maximum break of his career, having potted 15 reds and 15 blacks, but went in-off after colliding with the pink following an extremely delicate cut.

‌



O'Sullivan missed out on a possible £5,000 for highest break but felt sufficiently assured to seal the win without complications. He compiled an impressive 114 in frame six to wrap up victory.

He faces Matthew Selt next on Wednesday and will surely hope that any wardrobe malfunctions will have been sorted by then.

Elsewhere in the tournament, Judd Trump takes on Mark Lloyd, Zhao Xintong faces Fuyuan Han and Ricky Walden meets Xinbo Wang in the first round.

Article continues below

John Higgins, Xinbo Wang, Xiao Guodong and Zhang Anda all progressed to the second round. Stuart Bingham, Jack Lisowski, Elliot Slessor and Hossein Vafaei are all in action later today looking to seal their spot in the same round.

Sports.title: Jake Paul reveals four-man hitlist including Tommy Fury and Oleksandr Usyk
Sports.content:
Jake Paul reveals four-man hitlist including Tommy Fury and Oleksandr Usyk

The 'Problem Child' is looking to make a return to competitive action later this year after his brutal knockout loss to Anthony Joshua last December

View Image Jake Paul shakes hands with Trump on stage at Verst Logistics on March 11 (Image: Getty Images)

Jake Paul revealed his four-man hitlist to US President Donald Trump - which included a clash with Oleksandr Usyk and a rematch with bitter rival Tommy Fury.

‌



The 29-year-old has not fought since he was knocked out by Anthony Joshua in December. Paul is expected to make a return to the ring later this year after breaking his jaw in two places in the defeat to the former two-time heavyweight world champion.

‌



But despite his crushing defeat to the heavyweight behemoth, the 'Problem Child' is still eager to face some of the biggest stars in the combat sports world. The 29-year-old recently sat down with Trump and discussed his boxing career, as well as other topics.

‌



"So, who do you fight next?" Trump asked Paul, after admitting he watched the YouTuber-turned-boxers fight with in December while on board Air Force One. "Would you ever fight [Joshua] again?" The young American did not hesitate when feeding back his answer: "No, I don't think so.

FOLLOW OUR FIGHTING FB PAGE! Latest boxing and MMA news, analysis and much more via our dedicated Facebook page

Tyson Fury vs Anthony Joshua 'agreed' as stunning details emerge READ MORE :

Jake Paul brutally mocked over unfortunate situation on stage with Donald Trump READ MORE :

"I need to go and stay at my weight class. Maybe do some MMA—we're promoting fights now on Netflix; we got Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano." Despite his eagerness to move back down to cruiserweight, the 'Problem Child' is open to fighting both Usyk and former UFC heavyweight champion Francis Ngannou. "Maybe [Oleksandr] Usyk in MMA, Francis Ngannou, Ryan Garcia, maybe Tommy Fury —there's a lot."

‌



Paul then asked Trump who he'd like to see him face next. "Probably Khabib [Nurmagomedov]," Trump responded. A showdown between the pair seems unlikely, though, after the Russian UFC legend retired from competition back in 2020 after his dominant submission win over Justin Gaethje.

Mike Tyson vs Floyd Mayweather boxing fight to have unusual rule READ MORE :

Donald Trump endorses Jake Paul for political career amid injury update READ MORE :

Usyk has welcomed a fight with Paul in the cage in the past, but the Ukrainian is solely focused on his boxing career - having laid out his three-fight plan - for now as he enters the twilight stages of his career. Usyk will return to the ring in Egypt in May against kickboxing legend Rico Verhoeven. He then wants the winner of Fabio Wardley's upcoming fight with Daniel Dubois before taking on Tyson Fury for a third time.

The 'Problem Child' has been pleading for a fight with Fury's younger brother ever since their initial meeting back in 2023. The former Love Island contestant would go on to hand the American the first loss of his professional career following a split decision win in Saudi Arabia.

Article continues below

Boxing star Garcia admitted last year that he rejected $20m to fight Paul before the fight with Joshua came to fruition. A potential clash between Ngannou and Paul could be on the cards after the 'Predator' penned a deal with Most Valuable Promotions (MVP) and is set to fight on the Rousey vs Carano undercard against Philipe Lins.

Ngannou - like Garcia - turned down a fight with the YouTuber-turned-boxer, but now appears to have a change of heart. "You were just being a small boy, not understanding, being a little disrespectful, and that's why I want to kick your a** now," Ngannou told Paul. "I really didn't want to fight you, but now I want to beat you—so there's a difference. Let's do it. That's why I stand by the statement that I want to beat you up."

Sports.title: Lewis Hamilton F1 film wins Oscar as Ferrari star makes Hollywood decision
Sports.content:
Lewis Hamilton F1 film wins Oscar as Ferrari star makes Hollywood decision

Lewis Hamilton co-produced the F1 film which was nominated for four Oscars at the Hollywood ceremony, and the awards show took place hours after he finished in third at the Chinese Grand Prix

View 2 Images Lewis Hamilton had a weekend to remember including a third-placed finish in the Chinese Grand Prix. (Image: Anni Graf - Formula 1/Formula 1 via Getty Images)

Lewis Hamilton's 'F1: The Movie' won an Academy Award for Best Sound at the Oscars on Sunday night, but the co-producer was unable to attend due to the Chinese Grand Prix earlier in the day. The Brit earned his first podium in the red of Ferrari in Shanghai, behind Kimi Antonelli and George Russell.

‌



The film was nominated for four awards at the awards ceremony, including Best Picture and Visual Effects. As well as co-producing the box office hit, Hamilton enjoyed a cameo role alongside Brad Pitt and Damson Idris.

‌



Since its release in June 2025, it has become the highest-grossing sports film with a gross of over €630million (£545m) worldwide. After winning a Grammy last month with Chris Stapleton's 'Bad As I Used To Be' song and claiming a BAFTA for Best Sound, the moving picked up another award over the weekend.

‌



Jannik Sinner's F1 Chinese Grand Prix decision as Indian Wells winner reveals sacrifice made READ MORE :

While F1 missed out in the Best Picture, Best Film Editing and Best Visual Effects categories, it did win Best Sound.

Upon collecting the award, members of the cast and team made sure to give Hamilton a shoutout during their acceptance speech.

The success will be a cause for double celebration for Hamilton after his podium finish in China. It was his first time in the top three while driving for Ferrari.

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

It’s reported that Hamilton had explored ‘every way’ to try to attend the ceremony, but the timeline proved to be too difficult. Shanghai is a 12-and-a-half-hour flight from Los Angeles, making it impossible for him to get there in time.

Speaking earlier in the week, Hamilton said: "I'll FaceTime with Joe [Kosinski, director] and Jerry [Bruckheimer, lead producer] when they're there, which will be cool.

‌



View 2 Images The team behind F1: The Movie accept the Best Sound Oscar. (Image: Kevin Winter/Getty Images)

"I'm incredibly proud, and I never ever thought in a million years that that would be the outcome of the work that we're doing over the past years.

"It's amazing to see. I don't know if it's the sport, but to see how much promotion there is around the world, to see the buzz, to see new people getting excited for the sport, in the way that so many of us were growing up.

Article continues below

"It's really great to see that that is expanding. And on top of that, I'm still here, still to be a part of it and witness it."

Hamilton's third-place finish moves him up to fourth overall in the rankings. He will next be on the track in the Japanese Grand Prix on Sunday, 29 March.

Sports.title: Tyson Fury vs Anthony Joshua 'agreed' as stunning details emerge
Sports.content:
Tyson Fury vs Anthony Joshua 'agreed' as stunning details emerge

A showdown between the pair appeared to be off the cards following Joshua's near-death experience in December, but it has now been revealed that the fight could be back on

View Image Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua could fight this year

Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua have reportedly agreed to meet in a mouthwatering heavyweight showdown - as major details have been revealed.

‌



Back in December, reports emerged that the pair were due to finally settle their long-time feud in the ring in 2026. However, just days after his knockout win over Jake Paul this past Christmas, Joshua was involved in a tragic car crash in Nigeria, which resulted in the deaths of his two close friends Sina Ghami and Lateef 'Latz' Ayodele.

‌



There were fears that Joshua might not ever fight again after he cheated death, but the former two-time heavyweight world champion is now back doing some light training amid talks of a potential return to the ring this summer. 'AJ' has been mooted to face ex-rival Dillian Whyte before chasing a clash with Fury.

‌



According to boxing journalist Gareth A Davies, both Fury and Joshua have agreed to meet in the ring. Speaking on talkSPORT, Davies revealed that the fight is 'signed' before claiming it's been 'agreed'. "The Fury-Joshua fight is signed," he said. "Okay, it is signed in the background. I've got that on good authority."

FOLLOW OUR FIGHTING FB PAGE! Latest boxing and MMA news, analysis and much more via our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Jake Paul brutally mocked over unfortunate situation on stage with Donald Trump READ MORE :

Mike Tyson vs Floyd Mayweather boxing fight to have unusual rule READ MORE :

Davies added that the showcase will be live on Netflix and will likely take place either 'late in the year' or 'next year' - with Joshua likely to return to action in either 'June or July.' "It's with the big money people. It'll be on Netflix." Davies added: "It's on the cards and it's going to happen."

Netflix are due to air Fury's comeback on April 11. Last year, the 'Gypsy King' stunned fans after announcing his retirement from the sport of boxing, only to reverse his decision just months later. The 37-year-old is scheduled to take on dangerous Russian knockout artist Arslanbek Makhmudov at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in London. Netflix have emerged as a real powerhouse in the world of boxing in recent times, having aired showdowns such as Paul vs Joshua, Canelo Alvarez vs Terence Crawford, Katie Taylor vs Amanda Serrano and Paul vs Mike Tyson.

Donald Trump endorses Jake Paul for political career amid injury update READ MORE :

Joshua is is closing in on a return to full training ahead of a potential return to the squared-circle later this year. Last month, the Brit's promoter, Eddie Hearn had revealed that an all-British heavyweight showdown between Fury and Joshua could still happen despite his star client's brush with death.

Article continues below

"Originally the plan with AJ was for him to fight in March and then fight Tyson Fury in August," Hearn told Boxing Scene. "He's not going to fight Tyson Fury next; he's going to come back, I believe, in late summer. Physically he's not yet in a position to return so I'm planning and he's just resting and preparing.

"I'm looking at options to get him back in the ring in July but we will only know if that's a real possibility when he returns to camp which will hopefully be in the next couple of weeks Every fight is dangerous coming off what he's been through. We are open to the Tyson Fury fight but more likely at the end of the year or early in 2027."

Sports.title: Jannik Sinner's F1 Chinese Grand Prix decision as Indian Wells winner reveals sacrifice made
Sports.content:
Jannik Sinner's F1 Chinese Grand Prix decision as Indian Wells winner reveals sacrifice made

Jannik Sinner missed watching Kimi Antonelli win the Chinese Grand Prix as he beat Daniil Medvedev to win his first Indian Wells title

View 2 Images Jannik Sinner won at Indian Wells on Sunday. (Image: Getty Images)

Jannik Sinner admitted that he had to snub watching the Chinese Grand Prix to secure Indian Wells success. The world number two defeated Daniil Medvedev in straight sets to emulate Roger Federer and Novak Djokovic by completing the collection of hard court tournaments.

‌



However, the 24-year-old made the claim that he had to resist his urges and desires, like staying up late to watch the F1 in Shanghai as it would have affected his tennis. Sinner joined the exclusive group after previously winning the Australian Open and US Open, as well as all six Masters 1,000 titles before adding Indian Wells silverware to his, already, remarkable collection.

‌



The Italian required tiebreaks to overcome Medvedev and in the match-winning set, he disclosed that he had to shake off fatigue to power through.

‌



Carlos Alcaraz hates playing 'nightmare' Brit whose Wimbledon antics attracted fury READ MORE :

Tennis trio were banned for 37 years in total as new match-fixing scandal grips the sport READ MORE :

He said: "I tried to shake it off and after I was loose again. I held back a little bit so that's a point I would like to improve.

"I felt like I could have played slightly more aggressive. I wanted to watch the Formula One but it was midnight.

"I'm not capable of going to sleep very late and playing tennis the next day so very good discipline. Congrats Kimi (Antonelli)."

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

Sinner was forced to miss Kimi Antonelli claim maximum points in the second race of the season, beating Mercedes teammate George Russell - winner of the opener in Melbourne.

The Italian was forced to learn the result after the event in Shanghai as Los Angeles is 15 hours behind China.

‌



It was British-heavy podium with Lewis Hamilton continuing his return to form to take third place. Sinner, meanwhile, has recovered from Australian Open disappointment where he was knocked out in a semi-final thriller with Novak Djokovic.

He defeated Learner Tien then Alexander Zverev in the semi-final en route to his Indian Wells success, not dropping a set in his path to glory.

View 2 Images Jannik Sinner bounced back from his Australian Open disappointment, romping to glory at Indian Wells. (Image: Getty Images)

Article continues below

Sinner added: "It was a tough tournament. In my mind I knew this was the only hardcourt tournament of the big ones that I haven't won so I'm very happy how I handled it.

"Seeing Daniil playing at this level is important for tennis. I feel like when he plays at his best he's very tough to beat as we saw today, but I'm very happy.

“Tennis needs him. He has a very unique style of playing, and seeing him back at this level is great. He’s improving a lot and is very aggressive. He has two different game styles when he serves and when he returns, so you have to face that.”

Sports.title: Matt Fitzpatrick left heartbroken as "good friend" costs him £3.4million payday
Sports.content:
Matt Fitzpatrick left heartbroken as "good friend" costs him £3.4million payday

Matt Fitzpatrick had a classy response to his devastating loss at the Players Championship

View 2 Images Matt Fitzpatrick was dramatically denied victory at the Players Championship (Image: James Gilbert, Getty Images)

Matt Fitzpatrick narrowly missed out on The Players Championship trophy to his "good friend" and TGL teammate Cameron Young. It was a bitter pill to swallow but the British star responded to his disappointment with typical grace.

‌



Young birdied the 17th hole and then two-putted from 9 1⁄2 feet on the 18th green to secure the first place prize of $4.5 million (£3.4m) at the Players Championship at TPC Sawgrass. He was playing alongside TGL teammate Fitzpatrick, who had been leading Young until the 17th hole.

‌



Young clinched victory in a thrilling final hole, with his understated gesture to Fitzpatrick noticed just before the end. Despite the crushing defeat and having to contend with jeering from spectators outside the ropes, Fitzpatrick was full of admiration for the experience of playing alongside his friend, who offered a different perspective on the contentious behaviour of the fans.

‌



"I love Cam," Fitzpatrick said after his narrow defeat. "He's such a nice guy. He's so laid back; it's so nice. Always knew we were going to have a good day today, just obviously both comfortable around each other, and it was."

Rory McIlroy showed vulnerable side with 'heartbroken' admission after Players Championship READ MORE :

Speaking to NBC on the 18th green, Fitzpatrick added that the 28 year old American is also a great teammate. He still claimed a solo second place finish behind Young, good for a little over £2m.

"I've got a ton of respect for Cam," he continued. "He's a good friend and teammate in TGL as well," Fitzpatrick added.

‌



"We've gone really well. I knew it was going to be a nice pairing today. When you're coming down the stretch you want to be with someone that you're comfortable with and I felt we're gonna have a good day. It was exciting."

Young, who shattered Fitzpatrick's dreams with a birdie on the iconic island 17th hole and then triumphed with a par on the 18th, gave a shout out to his playing partner "He's a great guy," Young remarked after his friend missed an eight-foot par putt which would have led to a three-hole playoff.

View 2 Images Fitzpatrick congratulated Young after the win (Image: Eston Parker/ISI Photos, ISI Photos via Getty Images)

‌



"We've gotten to be around each other a decent bit through TGL, through our team, New York, and he is an excellent playing partner," the American added. "He plays quick. He's a very polite, very kind guy and enjoyable to talk to when you get the odd moment to share a few words walking around out there.

"So a very, very comfortable pairing for me, and I think you probably saw it with both of our golf. We had every right to both be nervous and found ourselves in some tricky spots, but I feel like for the both of us I think it was a comfortable one."

Level on the leaderboard heading into the 18th hole, the two golfers chatted to help calm their nerves. "We were just saying stuff, I think, to just kind of quiet our own minds," Young explained.

Article continues below

"In those moments there's a lot running through your head, and for me to have [caddie] Kyle, one of my best friends, on the bag sitting there, it's a really nice time to have something a little bit lighter in terms of conversation.

"I could have stared at my feet walking all the way down the fairway or we could have looked around and had a little bit of a laugh. I think at the moment option two was a good call."

Sports.title: Adam Peaty given reality check months after Olympic champion married Holly Ramsay
Sports.content:
Adam Peaty given reality check months after Olympic champion married Holly Ramsay

Triple Olympic champion Adam Peaty took part in his first long-course competition since the Paris 2024 Olympics

View 3 Images Adam Peaty and Holly Ramsay married in 2025 (Image: Kym Illman, Getty Images)

Adam Peaty experienced a reality check as he returned to competitive swimming for the first time since tying the knot with Holly Ramsay. The triple Olympic champion came fourth at the Edinburgh International Swim Meet in the men's 100m breaststroke, his speciality event.

‌



The 31 year old was fastest at the 50m turn. However, he lost pace on the final stretch, recording a time of 1:00.74.

‌



Filip Nowacki clinched the race in 1:00.26, with Gregory Butler (1:00.37) and Max Morgan (1:00.54) securing second and third respectively. Peaty was some way off his world record of 56.88, which he clocked at the 2019 World Championships in South Korea.

‌



Some rustiness was expected from Peaty, considering the length of his absence from competitive action. The race was his first long-course event since the 2024 Olympic Games in Paris, where he won silver.

Adam Peaty reignites sad family feud on Mother's Day as wife Holly gushes over her mum READ MORE :

Holly Ramsay and Adam Peaty give glimpse at marital home after aunt brands him a 'tool' READ MORE :

Peaty married Holly, the daughter of famed chef Gordon Ramsay, in a lavish ceremony at Bath Abbey in December. The nuptials were surrounded by rumours of a family dispute, which allegedly intensified after Peaty's mother, Caroline, wasn't invited to the bride's hen party.

Adam and Holly appear in the new Netflix documentary series 'Being Gordon Ramsay', which tracks the Michelin-starred chef over nine months as he prepares to open three restaurants, a bar and a cooking academy in London.

‌



View 3 Images Peaty returned to swimming action in Edinburgh (Image: Ian MacNicol, Getty Images)

The series followed the couple as they revealed their engagement to the Ramsay family and featured footage from their engagement party. In the second episode, Gordon praised his son-in-law, saying: "Holly's partner is an amazing young man called Adam Peaty. He's a gent, an absolute thoroughbred. To see her walk down the aisle is going to be pretty incredible."

Adam and Holly shared their engagement news on Instagram in September 2024. The episode showed the Ramsay family's reaction to the announcement.

‌



Gordon and his wife, Tana, discussed how they "couldn't be happier" to welcome Peaty and his son into the family. The Staffordshire swimmer was previously in a relationship with Eirianedd Munro and the pair share a five year old son called George-Anderson.

View 3 Images Adam and Holly appear in a new Gordon Ramsay documentary (Image: Mark Sutton - Formula 1, Formula 1 via Getty Images)

During the episode, Peaty recalled the "daunting" moment he sought Holly's parents' blessing to marry her, and said: "It turned into a very wonderful moment that I'll remember for the rest of my life."

Article continues below

Episode four covers the couple's engagement party, with Peaty disclosing that he knew he wanted to marry Holly as she "brings me a peace that I've never had in all aspects in my life".

Peaty, a two-time Olympic breaststroke champion, is scheduled to compete at two major events this summer, the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow and the European Championships in Paris. He recently visited Australia's Gold Coast for a training camp.

Sports.title: Gary Neville tears into Chelsea huddle ‘nonsense’ after Liam Rosenior complaints
Sports.content:
Gary Neville tears into Chelsea huddle ‘nonsense’ after Liam Rosenior complaints

Chelsea’s pre-match huddle was interrupted by referee Paul Tierney ahead of their defeat against Newcastle and Gary Neville has hit back at the Blues amid scathing comments from Liam Rosenior

View 2 Images Gary Neville has shared his verdict on Chelsea's complaints (Image: Getty Images)

Gary Neville has slammed Chelsea’s complaints over their failed pre-match huddle vs Newcastle as ‘nonsense’. The Blues attempted to get all of their players together at the centre-circle ahead of kick-off on Saturday, only to see their plans foiled by referee Paul Tierney.

‌



The official was stationed in the same spot ahead of the game and refused to move. Giving his take, former Manchester United star Neville claimed the gesture was simply performative anyway.

‌



“I thought it was extremely odd. Really weird," said Neville. "I've never been a fan of huddles.

‌



“If you've prepared for seven months of a season, four days before the game and then you need a huddle 10 seconds before kick-off to talk and motivate each other, you've done something wrong in your preparation.

"No words can help you, in my opinion. No words should be able to help you seconds before a football match. You've done all your preparations. You've analysed the player you are playing against. You know how to create opportunities if you're an attacking player.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Ashley Cole lands first job in management with shock switch to second-tier club READ MORE :

Liam Rosenior to hold urgent PGMOL talks after Chelsea referee incident in Newcastle loss READ MORE :

"You're in the changing room 10 minutes before you go out. You've said everything. What more can be said in a huddle out on the pitch? It's just for show.

"The fans won't be conned by that. They will judge you on your performance.

"It won't intimidate a good team. It didn't intimidate Newcastle. It's weird to do it in the centre of the pitch. It's like 'what's the most gimmicky thing we can do to make people believe we have team spirit?'

‌



"I think it's a nonsense. The whole thing is a nonsense. Stop doing it."

View 2 Images Referee Tierney got trapped in Chelsea's pre-match huddle (Image: Getty Images)

Chelsea boss Liam Rosenior was not best pleased with Tierney’s actions and confirmed he was set to get in touch with the PGMOL. Speaking after his side’s defeat to Newcastle, he said: "I didn't speak to Paul today or his officials. I thought it wasn't the right thing to do today. But I'll be speaking to PGMOL.

‌



"I'll be speaking to the refs and just trying to get an understanding of why that happened today. We were told in the rule book it's about timing. You can be where you want on timing.

"I just want to find a solution to this because we're actually talking about something that's nowhere near as important as what's happening on the pitch."
